      "slug": "summary-windows-ports-not-visible-wsl-ubuntu",
      "page_type": "summary",
      "title": "Windows Ports Not Visible WSL Ubuntu (summary)",
      "body": "This document outlines troubleshooting steps for local development port conflicts in WSL2, specifically addressing conflicts on ports 8080/8081. The root cause analysis synthesizes issues from [[hyper-v-port-exclusion]] and [[stale-service-worker-routing]]. It details a diagnostic strategy using Windows PowerShell commands, covers [[wsl2-networking-modes]] (NAT vs. mirrored mode), and details a safe port strategy in [[wsl2-localhost-port-conflict-troubleshooting]].",
      "status": "active",
      "outgoing_links": [
        "hyper-v-port-exclusion",
        "stale-service-worker-routing",
        "wsl2-networking-modes",
        "wsl2-localhost-port-conflict-troubleshooting"

      "confidence": "high"
    },
    {
      "slug": "hyper-v-port-exclusion",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "Hyper-V Port Exclusion",
      "body": "Hyper-V reserves specific port ranges for its own virtualization stack, acting as administrative or dynamic exclusions. In Windows, these exclusions appear under the System process (PID 4) inside `netstat` and can be checked using the command `netsh interface ipv4 show excludedportrange protocol=tcp`. These reservations are not active TCP sockets but kernel-level placeholders, meaning they prevent any other process from binding to them but cannot be observed by standard guest networking utilities.",
      "status": "active",
      "outgoing_links": [],
      "confidence": "high"
    },
    {
      "slug": "wsl2-networking-modes",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "WSL2 Networking Modes",
      "body": "WSL2 supports two primary networking modes:\n1. **NAT Mode (Default):** WSL2 operates with its own virtual network adapter isolated from the Windows host. Linux utilities like `ss` and `lsof` can only enumerate Linux kernel sockets and are blind to host-side sockets and exclusions.\n2. **Mirrored Mode (`networkingMode=mirrored`):** WSL2 shares the same network namespace as the Windows host. This allows Windows-side active sockets to mirror and show up inside WSL2. However, mirrored mode only mirrors actual active bound sockets, not placeholder reservations such as [[hyper-v-port-exclusion]].",
      "status": "active",
      "outgoing_links": [
        "hyper-v-port-exclusion"
      ],
      "confidence": "high"
    },
    {
      "slug": "stale-service-worker-routing",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "Stale Service Worker Routing",
      "body": "A registered service worker under an origin like `localhost:8080` can intercept and hijack browser requests, returning cached or routing responses (e.g. React Router errors) even when the backend files have changed. Hard refreshes do not clear a registered service worker; they must be manually unregistered in browser DevTools under the Application or Service Workers tab.",
      "status": "active",
      "outgoing_links": [],
      "confidence": "high"
    },
    {
      "slug": "wsl2-localhost-port-conflict-troubleshooting",
      "page_type": "article",
      "title": "WSL2 Localhost Port Conflict Troubleshooting",
      "body": "A confounding issue occurs when a local development port in WSL2 appears completely available inside Linux, yet acts abnormally or fails to respond in a browser. This usually points to a multi-layered failure involving both the host and the browser environment. In this scenario, [[hyper-v-port-exclusion]] blocks ports from the Windows side (making them appear bound or inaccessible to external browsers), while a [[stale-service-worker-routing]] interceptor in the browser returns cached assets or mock routing paths (such as a router 404 block) rather than communicating with the active WSL2 server. Because WSL2 defaults to NAT in its [[wsl2-networking-modes]], running commands like `ss` or `lsof` inside WSL will fail to show host port issues. Diagnostics must be run from Windows PowerShell using `netstat -ano` and `netsh` to identify administrative reservations. Resolving this conflict requires selecting a safe, non-excluded port (such as 8082) and unregistering conflicting service workers inside browser DevTools.",
      "status": "active",
      "outgoing_links": [
        "hyper-v-port-exclusion",
        "stale-service-worker-routing",
        "wsl2-networking-modes"
      ],
      "confidence": "high"
    }
